name: Ferro & Fiamma
philosophy: Old-school Naples meets industrial Brooklyn. We are obsessive about sourcing — San Marzano DOP tomatoes, fior di latte from a local creamery, 72-hour sourdough fermentation. Every pizza is wood-fired to charred perfection. No shortcuts, no compromises, just fire and iron.

logo.clearSpace: Maintain clear space equal to the height of the ampersand on all sides
donts: Never place the logo on busy photographic backgrounds without a solid backing
Never distort, rotate, or recolor the logo outside approved palette

primary.name: Fornace Red
primary.role: Primary brand — headlines, CTAs, key accents. The deep red of a wood-fired oven interior.

primary.name: Charcoal
primary.role: Text, dark backgrounds, footer. The char on the crust.

primary.name: Warm Cream
primary.role: Page backgrounds, light sections. The color of fresh dough.

secondary.name: Olive Grove
secondary.role: Accent — badges, secondary buttons, subtle highlights. Fresh basil and olive oil.

secondary.name: Terracotta
secondary.role: Warm accent — highlights, hover states, decorative elements.

heading.usage: Page titles, hero headlines, section headings. Evokes old Italian signage.

subheading.usage: Section subtitles, card headings, menu category names

body.usage: Body copy, descriptions, paragraphs. Clean and legible.

eyebrow.usage: Section labels, category tags, overlines. Spaced at 0.12em.

rules: Headings always use Playfair Display — never Source Sans 3
Line height: 1.2 for headings, 1.6 for body

photography.style: Moody, warm, high-contrast. Close-up food shots with shallow depth of field. Warm tungsten lighting with deep shadows. Industrial textures — exposed brick, marble counters, weathered steel. Authentic kitchen action, not staged perfection.
rules: Always warm white balance — no cool/blue tones
Show texture: bubbling cheese, charred crust, flour-dusted surfaces
Include environmental shots: the oven mouth glowing, hands stretching dough
People shots are candid — staff at work, guests mid-bite, never posed
Depth of field: subject sharp, background soft and warm
donts: No stock photo aesthetic — nothing that looks mass-produced
No cold blue or fluorescent lighting
photography.social: Instagram-first: close-ups of the pizza-making process, oven shots, ingredient details. Stories show behind-the-scenes kitchen moments.

voice.character: Confident, warm, and unpretentious. We know our craft inside out but never lecture. Like your favorite Italian uncle who takes food seriously but doesn't take himself seriously.

do.text: 72 hours. That's how long our dough ferments before it ever sees the oven.
do.context: Leads with the compelling detail, lets the craft speak for itself

do.text: San Marzano DOP. Not San Marzano style. Not San Marzano inspired. The real thing.
do.context: Direct, confident, makes the sourcing standard clear without being preachy

do.text: Wood-fired at 900°F. Sixty seconds. That's all it takes when the dough is right.
do.context: Short punchy sentences convey mastery and confidence

do.text: Come hungry. Leave happy. It's not complicated.

dont.text: We are proud to offer an artisanal dining experience featuring locally-sourced ingredients.
dont.context: Corporate buzzwords — 'artisanal', 'dining experience', 'locally-sourced' are empty calories

dont.text: Our award-winning chef has crafted a menu that celebrates the rich tapestry of Neapolitan tradition.
dont.context: Overwrought and pretentious — we show, we don't brag

dont.text: Pizza is life! 🍕🔥 Come get your slice!!!
dont.context: Too casual, too many exclamation marks, emoji-heavy — we're warm, not wacky

rules: No more than one exclamation mark per page
Italian terms are fine when they're the real name (DOP, fior di latte, Margherita) — never for decoration
Short sentences. Let the periods do the work.
Always concrete over abstract — '900°F for 60 seconds' beats 'perfectly cooked'

tone.description: Tone shifts by context but always stays warm and grounded. On the homepage we're inviting and confident. On the menu we're direct and appetizing. On About Us we're storytelling — personal, a little nostalgic, deeply passionate.

writingGoals.description: Make people hungry and curious. Lead with sensory details — the smell of the oven, the sound of the crust, the sight of bubbling mozzarella.
Share the craft without lecturing. Our sourcing, our process, our standards — stated plainly, not defensively.
Build a relationship. We're a neighborhood place. We know your name, your order, your kids.

cleverVsClear.clever: Menu item descriptions can have personality. Section headings can be evocative ('From the Oven' not 'Pizza Menu').
cleverVsClear.clear: Hours, location, allergen info, ordering instructions — always crystal clear, never buried in prose.

contentPillars.name: The Craft
contentPillars.description: Our process: 72-hour fermentation, wood-fired technique, handmade from scratch daily

contentPillars.name: The Source
contentPillars.description: Where our ingredients come from: San Marzano DOP, local creamery fior di latte, small-batch olive oil

contentPillars.name: The Place
contentPillars.description: Brooklyn neighborhood culture — the regulars, the block, the community table

personas.name: The Purist
personas.description: Knows the difference between Neapolitan and New York style. Orders the Margherita to test a new spot. Cares about sourcing, fermentation, char level.
personas.motto: If the Margherita isn't right, nothing else matters.

stats.description: of our regulars — they came for the craft and stayed

personas.name: The Neighbor
personas.description: Lives on the block. Comes in twice a week. Orders the same thing every time. Knows the staff by name.

personas.name: The Explorer
personas.description: Found us on Instagram or a food blog. Wants to try the specials, photograph everything, tell their friends.
personas.motto: Have you tried that new pizza place in Brooklyn?

stats.description: of traffic — discovery-driven, high social share rate
